_meta.purpose: Hand-gathered FULL four-year BSCS/EECS graduation requirements per UC campus, modeled in the ASSIST agreement requirement_groups shape so the degree-coverage figure (MA Fig. 1) can reuse the existing choose-N eligibility engine. Course codes are resolved to university_courses.parent_id by scripts/import_uc_degree_requirements.py; provenance for every datum lives in docs/figures/degree-coverage-sources.md.

campus_key -> object.school_id: UC source id (matches assist_institutions.source_id and assist_agreements.uc_school_id)
campus_key -> object.program: the exact degree program these requirements describe
campus_key -> object.total_units: minimum units for graduation (secondary; the engine counts course-slots)
campus_key -> object.source_url: primary catalog URL for the program (details per-datum in the source-of-truth doc)

requirements[].from: one of: [course codes] (explicit courses, resolved to parent_id) | {series: [[codes...], ...]} (choose `select` complete series — 'in its entirety, no mixing'; a series articulates only when every course in it does) | {departments:[...], max_number:N} (proxy list: all lower-div courses in these depts) | null (non-transferable slots — never satisfiable by any CC)
requirements[].units: optional — the block's stated total units (e.g. Berkeley's 20-unit UD rule, a 12-unit series), stored as unit_advisement; the page's unit-budget check otherwise assumes a flat ~4u/course

notes: tier=transferable: real lower-division courses a CC can articulate (has a parent_id).
tier=breadth: H/SS / natural-science that transfers near-universally via breadth articulation; modeled as select-N from a department proxy list (v1). Refinement TODO: split R&C (2) from breadth (4).

tier=nontransferable: upper-division / residency / free electives — no CC equivalent by construction (ASSIST never articulates UC upper-division). Rendered as kind='requirement' receivers with no parent_id, so they always count against the denominator and are never satisfiable.
EECS<->ELENG alias: our scraped data still stores EECS 16A/16B; the current Berkeley catalog calls these ELENG 66/64. The importer aliases them, so codes may be authored either way.

Unresolved course codes in a from-list (honors variants, brand-new courses with no ASSIST receiving record) are KEPT by the importer as named never-satisfiable slots — author the full requirement as the catalog states it; articulability is the data's job, not the author's filter.
